description: A structurally invalid receipt that escapes INVALID classification by having a non-empty 'signature' field but no signer_pubkey_sha256. classify_trust() at decision_receipt_trust.py:149 routes to UNVERIFIABLE before the validator is called. receipt_id is null, ts is garbage, decision is forged — none evaluated.

receipt.injected_field: arbitrary garbage payload that would be rejected by any schema validator

attack_mechanics.step_1: classify_trust() called. receipt.get('signature') returns non-empty base64 string.
attack_mechanics.step_2: sig is non-empty, passes whitespace check.
attack_mechanics.step_4: Condition at line 149: sig is not None AND pubkey is None → return UNVERIFIABLE.
attack_mechanics.step_5: Validator (line 156) is never called. Structural invalidity is never evaluated.
attack_mechanics.step_6_risk: Any consumer gating on state != INVALID accepts this as a non-INVALID receipt. receipt_id=null, forged decision, garbage timestamp all go undetected.

expected_classify_trust_result.note: UNVERIFIABLE correctly returns is_well_formed=False. The risk is callers that gate on state != INVALID instead of is_well_formed.

conformance_requirement: All consumers of classify_trust() MUST gate on is_well_formed or is_trustworthy, never on state != INVALID. An audit of all call sites should confirm this. The UNVERIFIABLE state is correct per design; the vulnerability is in callers.
